title: Polyamine Modulation in Drinking Water
author: Ana Catarina Carapinha Cascalho
materials: Weigh the right amount of DFMO.

Spermine tetrahydrochloride; (Thermo Scientific; J63060; CAS: 306-67-2; MW: 348.18 g/mol) (Spermine) Spermidine trihydrochloride (Thermo Scientific; 215100250; CAS: 334-50-9; MW: 254.63 g/mol) (Spermidine)

steps.title: Drinking water working concentration is 2...
steps.instruction: Drinking water working concentration is 2 Mass / % volume in drinking water ( 20 g / 1 L ). Example: Dissolve 20 g DFMO in 1 L water.

steps.title: Dissolve the weighed DFMO in the correct volume of water
steps.instruction: Dissolve the weighed DFMO in the correct volume of water.

steps.title: Mix it by gently inverting and...
steps.instruction: Mix it by gently inverting and wait for the dissolution of the substance in water.

steps.title: Filter the drinking water through a 0
steps.instruction: Filter the drinking water through a 0.20 µm filter unit (Fisher Scientific; 15933307).

steps.title: Store the drinking water at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Store the drinking water at 4 °C . Renew the drinking bottles every 2 weeks.

steps.title: Day 21
steps.instruction: Day 21: Wean and separate male and female pups (no single mice to avoid stress/anxiety). Pups are then moved to drinking water delivery of compound. Drinking bottles are weighted to monitor the amount of consumed water. Do not forget to make a clear card and note on bottle so animal caretakers do not change the water in the facility

steps.title: Calculate the amount of substance, depending...
steps.instruction: Calculate the amount of substance, depending on the solvent volume, you have to add to reach a concentration of 0.75 Molarity (M) . Formula: Example: I need 25 mL of 0.75 M Spermidine Trihydrochloride stock. (Molecular Weight = 254.63 g/mol).

steps.title: The pure powder stocks are stored at 4 °C
steps.instruction: The pure powder stocks are stored at 4 °C. Weigh the necessary amount with a precision scale, in the appropriate recipient. Seal the container with parafilm. Write down the opening date if the compound was new. Register on the lab book the open date, Ref# and Lot# of every stock solution prepared.

steps.title: Add with a pro-pipettor a volume of solvent
steps.instruction: Add with a pro-pipettor a volume of solvent.

steps.title: Mix it (by gently inverting) and...
steps.instruction: Mix it (by gently inverting) and wait for the dissolution of the substance in water.

steps.title: Filter the solution into a fresh...
steps.instruction: Filter the solution into a fresh labelled falcon using a 0.22 µm filter (Carl Roth; SE2M035I07) connected to a syringe.

steps.title: Prepare and label aliquoting tubes
steps.instruction: Prepare and label aliquoting tubes: name and concentration of the solution (Spermidine or spermine 25 mg/ml; 10X stock) date of preparation name of the lab technician

steps.title: Aliquot the solution using the pre-defined volumes
steps.instruction: Aliquot the solution using the pre-defined volumes: If preweaning treatment: 400 µL /aliquot If postweaning treatments: 4 mL /aliquot If there is leftover volume aliquot it into a separate tube and clearly label the specific volume.

steps.title: Under the hood, you are going...
steps.instruction: Under the hood, you are going to replace the air into a nitrogen atmosphere to reduce amine degradation.

steps.title: Carefully open the lid of the...
steps.instruction: Carefully open the lid of the tube, place the pipette tip into the tube on the side without touching the liquid. Wait for 00:00:10 . Carefully and quickly close the cap. Repeat for all aliquots.

steps.title: Transfer aliquots to a box at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Transfer aliquots to a box at -20 °C . Keep the spermine and spermidine stocks up to 3 months.

steps.title: Check if you filled in all...
steps.instruction: Check if you filled in all the information in the template stock preparation, which must be present in the lab book.

steps.title: Prepare the drinking water for the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the drinking water for the mice by diluting the 0.75 Molarity (M) stock 1:250 ( 4 mL stock in 1 L tap water). Filter the drinking water through a 0.20 µm filter unit. Store the drinking water at 4 °C Drinking bottles should be renewed every 2 weeks.
